message.content:
{
  "homework": {
    "sessionInformation": {
      "sessionNumber": "1",
      "sessionTitle": "A Truly Beautiful Mind",
      "subject": "English Language and Literature",
      "grade": "Class IX / 9",
      "difficultyLevel": "Balanced",
      "learningPace": "Supportive",
      "estimatedHomeworkDuration": "45 minutes"
    },
    "homework": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "type": "Reading Comprehension & Analysis",
        "title": "Understanding the Life of Albert Einstein",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Identify key biographical details", "Analyze character traits"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Childhood of Einstein", "Personal life and marriage", "Scientific contributions"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 5,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Read the text 'A Truly Beautiful Mind' carefully. Answer the following questions based on your understanding of Einstein's life as portrayed in the lesson.",
        "question": "Based on the text, describe three qualities that made Albert Einstein a 'truly beautiful mind'. Support your answer with specific examples from his childhood or adult life mentioned in the passage.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write your response in 3-4 sentences.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "Students should identify qualities such as curiosity, kindness, humility, and dedication to science. Examples may include his childhood fascination with the compass, his simple lifestyle despite fame, or his humanitarian efforts."
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "type": "Vocabulary in Context",
        "title": "Words that Define a Genius",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Expand vocabulary", "Understand word usage"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Key vocabulary from the lesson", "Contextual meaning"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 4,
        "estimatedTime": "8 minutes",
        "instructions": "Find words in the text that match the following definitions. Then, write a new sentence for each word.",
        "question": "Match the words from the lesson to their meanings and create a sentence:\n1. A person who is very interested in learning new things.\n2. To make something clear or easy to understand.\n3. A feeling of deep respect and admiration.\n4. To continue doing something despite difficulties.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "List the word, its meaning from the text, and your own sentence.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "1. Curious / 2. Clarify / 3. Admiration / 4. Persist. Students must provide correct definitions from the context and original sentences."
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "type": "Creative Writing",
        "title": "A Letter to Einstein",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Express ideas creatively", "Connect with text themes"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Einstein's values", "Personal reflection"],
        "difficulty": "High",
        "marks": 6,
        "estimatedTime": "15 minutes",
        "instructions": "Imagine you are a student who has just read about Albert Einstein. Write a letter to him expressing your admiration for his scientific achievements and his simple, humble lifestyle.",
        "question": "Write a formal letter to Dr. Albert Einstein thanking him for his contributions to science and sharing how his life inspires you to be curious and kind.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Follow the standard format of a formal letter (Sender's Address, Date, Salutation, Body, Complimentary Close, Signature).",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "A well-structured letter with correct format, expressing genuine appreciation for Einstein's scientific work and personal character, linking back to themes in the lesson."
      },
      {
        "id": 4,
        "type": "Critical Thinking & Application",
        "title": "Science Beyond the Classroom",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["Apply knowledge to real life", "Evaluate impact"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Impact of science", "Humanitarian values"],
        "difficulty": "High",
        "marks": 5,
        "estimatedTime": "12 minutes",
        "instructions": "The lesson mentions that Einstein was not just a scientist but also a humanitarian. Discuss how scientific knowledge can be used for the betterment of society.",
        "question": "In your own words, explain how Albert Einstein's life demonstrates that a 'beautiful mind' is one that uses intelligence for the welfare of humanity. Give one example from the text and one real-life example of science helping people.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write a paragraph of 5-6 sentences.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "Students should connect Einstein's refusal to use his knowledge for war (e.g., the atomic bomb context) or his advocacy for peace with the idea of using intelligence for good. Real-life examples could include medical research, clean energy, or education initiatives."
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "totalQuestions": 4,
      "totalMarks": 20,
      "estimatedCompletionTime": "45 minutes",
      "learningOutcomesCovered": [
        "Identify key biographical details",
        "Analyze character traits",
        "Expand vocabulary",
        "Understand word usage",
        "Express ideas creatively",
        "Connect with text themes",
        "Apply knowledge to real life",
        "Evaluate impact"
      ],
      "topicsCovered": [
        "Childhood of Einstein",
        "Personal life and marriage",
        "Scientific contributions",
        "Key vocabulary from the lesson",
        "Contextual meaning",
        "Einstein's values",
        "Personal reflection",
        "Impact of science",
        "Humanitarian values"
      ],
      "subtopicsCovered": [
        "Curiosity and kindness in childhood",
        "Simple lifestyle vs. fame",
        "Vocabulary definitions and usage",
        "Letter writing format",
        "Science for humanitarian causes"
      ],
      "taskDistribution": {
        "Reading Comprehension & Analysis": 1,
        "Vocabulary in Context": 1,
        "Creative Writing": 1,
        "Critical Thinking & Application": 1
      },
      "homeExperimentIncluded": false,
      "parentEngagementIncluded": false
    }
  }
}
